current_plan.description: A full-stack application allowing users to create, read, update, and delete todo items using a Flask REST API and a vanilla JavaScript frontend.
current_plan.architecture: Client-Server architecture using Flask for the REST API backend, SQLite for persistence, and vanilla JavaScript/HTML/CSS for the frontend.

files.purpose: Central configuration file for the application, including database URI and secret keys.
Defines the SQLAlchemy model for the Todo item (e.g., task_id, content, completed).
The main Flask application entry point. Initializes the database, sets up the API routes (CRUD operations), and serves the API.
The main HTML structure for the application, linking CSS and JavaScript.
Basic CSS styling for the ToDo list interface.
Client-side logic. Handles fetching data from the Flask API, rendering the list, and managing user interactions (add, toggle, delete).

subtasks.name: Setup and Configuration
subtasks.description: Initialize the project structure and define shared settings.

subtasks.name: Backend API Development (Database & Models)
subtasks.description: Define the database model and initialize the Flask application structure.

subtasks.name: Backend API Implementation (Routes)
subtasks.description: Implement the CRUD endpoints (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) in the Flask API.

subtasks.name: Frontend Setup and Styling
subtasks.description: Create the basic HTML structure and apply initial CSS styling.

subtasks.name: Frontend Logic and Integration
subtasks.description: Implement the client-side JavaScript logic to interact with the Flask API and render the ToDo list.
